message:
Unify BeadsView render tree so Script tags persist across state transitions

Previously BeadsView had two completely separate render paths: an early-return
loading state and the main loaded state, each with their own <Script> tags for
sql-wasm.js, d3.v7.min.js, and force-graph.min.js. When the component
transitioned from loading→loaded, React would unmount the loading tree (and its
Script elements) then mount the loaded tree (with fresh Script elements). This
could cause onLoad callbacks to be lost during the transition, leaving the
scripts in a zombie state where the global was available but the React state
flag was never set.

Fix: Collapse into a single return with {loading ? <spinner/> : <content/>}
inside a shared wrapper that always renders the three Script tags at the top
level. Scripts mount once and stay mounted for the component's lifetime.

Additional fixes in this change:
- Added d3Loaded to the renderGraph guard condition and its dependency array,
  preventing force-graph from attempting to render before D3 is available
  (D3 is required for force-graph's internal force simulation)
- Bumped graph container fallback height from 600px to 650px for better
  vertical coverage on standard viewports
- Added text-left to activity log, ledger items, and modal containers to
  prevent inherited center-alignment from parent sections
- Removed redundant inline comments, collapsed short single-child JSX
  onto single lines for density

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
